Testam</TITLE>, in einer Connex. mit der V√∂lcker-Historie, <PLACE>Berlin</PLACE> <YEAR>1725</YEAR>. <VOLUME>4. 1I. Tom. 1. Vol. 2</VOLUME> √† <AUTHOR>Cattenburg</AUTHOR></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Sapientia Mos. de Vita & Scriptis Mosis contra Deistas</TITLE>. <PLACE>Amst.</PLACE> <YEAR>1737</YEAR>. <FORMAT>4.</FORMAT></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Bibl.</TITLE> <VOLUME>8r. 70.</VOLUME> <AUTHOR>Interpp. Reineccii</AUTHOR>, <PLACE>Lips.</PLACE> <YEAR>1737</YEAR>. <FORMAT>8√≤.</FORMAT></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Bibl. Hebr.</TITLE> von <AUTHOR>punctata Leusdenii</AUTHOR>, <PLACE>Frf.</PLACE> <YEAR>1694</YEAR>. <FORMAT>12.</FORMAT></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Seldenus</AUTHOR> <TITLE>de Diis Syris</TITLE>. <PLACE>Lips.</PLACE> <YEAR>1692</YEAR>. <FORMAT>8.</FORMAT></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Gottschedens</AUTHOR> <TITLE>critische Dichtkunst</TITLE>, <PLACE>Leipzig</PLACE> <YEAR>1737</YEAR>. <FORMAT>8v√≤.</FORMAT></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>B√∂dickers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Grunds√§tze der Deutschen Sprach</TITLE>, <PLACE>Berlin</PLACE>, <YEAR>1729</YEAR>. <FORMAT>8v√≤.</FORMAT></BIBL></ITEM>","number_of_corrections":3,"explanation":"Three corrections were made: (1) The tag <PRIZE>Cattenburg</PRIZE> was changed to <AUTHOR>Cattenburg</AUTHOR>, since \"Cattenburg\" (Adrian van Cattenburgh) is the author/editor of the work, not a price — the preceding \"à\" introduces the author attribution, not a monetary value. (2) The second BIBL element (\"Sapientia Mos. de Vita & Scriptis Mosis contra Deistas ... Amst. 1737. 4.\") was missing its closing </BIBL> tag before the next <BIBL> opened, which caused invalid nesting of BIBL elements; the closing tag was added after the <FORMAT>4.</FORMAT> element. (3) As a consequence of fix 2, the third bibliographic entry (\"Bibl. ... Interpp. Reineccii, Lips. 1737\") is now a properly separated sibling BIBL element rather than being incorrectly nested inside the previous one, making the XML well-formed and structurally consistent with the other entries in the advert."}

parsed.explanation: Three corrections were made: (1) The tag <PRIZE>Cattenburg</PRIZE> was changed to <AUTHOR>Cattenburg</AUTHOR>, since "Cattenburg" (Adrian van Cattenburgh) is the author/editor of the work, not a price — the preceding "à" introduces the author attribution, not a monetary value. (2) The second BIBL element ("Sapientia Mos. de Vita & Scriptis Mosis contra Deistas ... Amst. 1737. 4.") was missing its closing </BIBL> tag before the next <BIBL> opened, which caused invalid nesting of BIBL elements; the closing tag was added after the <FORMAT>4.</FORMAT> element. (3) As a consequence of fix 2, the third bibliographic entry ("Bibl. ... Interpp. Reineccii, Lips. 1737") is now a properly separated sibling BIBL element rather than being incorrectly nested inside the previous one, making the XML well-formed and structurally consistent with the other entries in the advert.
